title: Establishing a baseline
scope: You can't judge features without one. Defines how to set a minimal, simple model and feature set as a reference point to measure the actual contribution of newly engineered features.
key_concepts: A baseline model provides a reference point for evaluating all subsequent feature engineering efforts.
It uses simple, readily available features without complex transformations.
Every new feature or complex representation must justify its added cost by outperforming the baseline.
definitions: Baseline: A reference performance metric achieved by a simple, interpretable model using basic features.
examples: Using raw numerical variables and simple one-hot encoding for categorical variables as a starting point before trying target encoding or deep embeddings.
relations_to_other_chapters: Acts as the prerequisite step before conducting ablation studies (s03) or checking SHAP values (s05).
